title: rewrite_outgoing
description: Rewrite an outgoing message toward a target register while preserving caller-named technical terms and the message's underlying intent. Returns the rewritten text, a verified list of preserved terms, and a short diff summary. The server orchestrates a structured prompt against the caller's MCP-client LLM; the server itself performs NO LLM calls and stores NO inputs.

text.description: The verbatim outgoing message to rewrite. Stored only in-memory for the duration of the call; never logged.
examples: This is broken. Please fix before EOD.
Strong nack. The approach in section 3 will not scale.
I'll loop back next week with thoughts on the architecture doc.

target_register.description: Required target register for the rewrite. Per plan.md §7 acceptance, rewrites MUST preserve all technical terms.

preserve_terms.description: Caller-supplied list of terms (technical jargon, names, system identifiers, ticket ids) that MUST appear unchanged in the rewritten text. Each term is checked by exact substring match after rewrite; missing terms appear in unpreserved_terms in the output. Capped at 100.

channel.description: Optional channel hint. Influences register expectations and acceptable length.

preserve_intent.description: When true (default), the rewrite preserves the message's underlying ask or position even if surface phrasing softens. When false, the caller is explicitly allowing semantic shift; rarely needed and SHOULD be left at the default.

rewritten.description: The rewritten message in the target register. The 12000-character ceiling is wider than the input ceiling to permit explanatory expansion (e.g. when shifting from 'concise' to 'clarifying'). The caller is responsible for choosing whether to present this as a one-click replace or as a side-by-side suggestion.

preserved_terms.description: Terms from input.preserve_terms that the server verified are present in rewritten via exact substring match. Order matches input order.

unpreserved_terms.description: Terms from input.preserve_terms that were NOT found verbatim in rewritten. Empty array when all terms were preserved. A non-empty array is a soft signal that the caller MAY want to retry or surface the gap to the user; the server does NOT auto-retry.

diff_summary.description: Short structured summary of what changed. Used by the extension to render an 'I changed: X, Y, Z' explanation rather than a raw diff.

eval_corpus_slice.description: Identifier of the eval corpus slice that validates this prompt template. Required so that any prompt change can be re-evaluated in CI per plan.md §7.

model_provenance.description: Where the LLM call ran (local or cloud, model id). Surfaced so the user / extension can render the 'cloud mode' banner accurately.

TEXT_REQUIRED.description: The text argument was missing or empty.

TEXT_TOO_LONG.description: Input text exceeded the 8000-character cap. Caller SHOULD split or excerpt before calling.

TARGET_REGISTER_REQUIRED.description: target_register was missing. Use one of: direct, warm, formal, concise, clarifying.

TARGET_REGISTER_UNKNOWN.description: target_register was not one of the v0.1.0 enum values.

PRESERVE_TERMS_TOO_MANY.description: preserve_terms exceeded 100 entries. Most messages should not approach this; if you do, you may be overusing the tool.

MODEL_UNAVAILABLE.description: The caller's MCP client could not reach its configured LLM.

examples.name: blunt PR comment rewritten toward 'direct' (technical, not aggressive)

input.text: Strong nack. The approach in section 3 will not scale and we need to revisit before merge.

output.rewritten: I think section 3 is going to hit a scaling limit — I'd like to revisit before we merge. Happy to walk through the specific concern in a comment or a quick call, whichever is easier.

diff_summary.tone_shift: Replaced 'Strong nack' (blunt rejection token) with a specific technical concern; preserved the merge-blocker signal but framed as collaborative.
structural_changes: Added offer to walk through the concern
Preserved technical claim and merge-blocker intent

examples.name: EOD ask rewritten toward 'warm'; preserves EOD verbatim

input.text: This is broken. Please fix before EOD.

output.rewritten: Hey — heads up that this is broken on my end. Any chance you can land a fix by EOD? Happy to help debug if it would speed things up.

diff_summary.tone_shift: Lifted warmth by opening with relational acknowledgement and offering help; preserved the EOD deadline and the broken-state report.

examples.name: rewrite drops a preserve term; server surfaces the gap rather than retrying

input.text: We should swap fastembed-py for sqlite-vec on the alias-resolution path.

output.rewritten: Swap fastembed-py for sqlite-vec on the alias path.

diff_summary.tone_shift: Compressed to a single imperative sentence in the 'concise' register.
warnings: Term 'alias-resolution' was not preserved verbatim. Caller may retry with stricter instruction or surface the gap to the user.

compatibility.policy: Additive-only within v0.1.x. New optional input fields (e.g. style_examples, max_length) MAY land in v0.1.x. New target_register enum values are minor bumps (read-only enum; callers MUST treat unknown values as opaque). Renaming or retyping any field requires a new major version with a parallel $id.
compatibility.side_effects: None. Pure read. The server stores no input, emits no telemetry, and does NOT replace text in the user's editor — that is the caller's (extension's) responsibility.
compatibility.idempotency: Server-side idempotent. LLM-side determinism depends on the caller's model and sampling settings; repeated calls MAY produce different rewrites.
compatibility.privacy: local-only-by-default. The server itself does not call any provider. Cloud-mode is selected by the caller's MCP client and the user's profile.

tone_shift.description: One-sentence plain-language description of the tone change.

structural_changes.description: Short bullet list of concrete structural changes the rewrite made.

warnings.description: Soft warnings the caller MAY want to surface to the user. Includes per-term preservation failures and intent-drift warnings. Empty array when no warnings.
